idea.topic: Supermassive Black Hole Event Horizon vs. Solar System
idea.hook: What if a BLACK HOLE was BIGGER than our ENTIRE SOLAR SYSTEM?!
facts: Our Solar System, from the Sun to Pluto's average orbit, spans about 80 AU (Astronomical Units) in diameter.
The supermassive black hole TON 618 boasts an event horizon with a staggering diameter of ~1740 AU.
This means TON 618's event horizon is over 21 times wider than our entire Solar System out to Pluto.
idea.payoff: Its colossal event horizon could easily swallow our Sun, all planets, the Kuiper Belt, and even the inner Oort Cloud within its grasp!
idea.title: Black Hole vs. Solar System: SHOCKING Scale! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: What if a BLACK HOLE was BIGGER than our ENTIRE SOLAR SYSTEM?!
scenes.visual: A distant view of our Solar System (Sun, planets) fades in, then a menacing black hole silhouette begins to emerge from the side, growing larger.
An animated infographic of our Solar System, showing the Sun, all planets, and Pluto's average orbit clearly marked, with '80 AU' highlighted.

scenes.text: Black Hole TON 618: ~1740 AU event horizon
scenes.visual: A visualization of the supermassive black hole TON 618, with a prominent, glowing event horizon. '1740 AU' text appears near it, emphasizing its scale.

scenes.text: TON 618 is 21x WIDER than our Solar System!
scenes.visual: A stark comparison: the entire Solar System graphic (from Scene 2, scaled down to a tiny dot) is placed next to the massive TON 618 event horizon, which dwarfs it. A '21x' multiplier animates between them.

scenes.text: It could SWALLOW our Sun, planets, & inner Oort Cloud!
scenes.visual: The gigantic black hole TON 618's event horizon dominates the screen. Our entire Solar System, represented as a tiny, distant cluster of light, appears to be inexorably drawn into the black hole and disappears within its darkness.

youtube.title: Black Hole vs. Solar System: SHOCKING Scale! 🤯🌌
